title: Redaction Config Schema
description: JSON Schema for the `security.redact` config block introduced in #692 PR-B. The shape mirrors `redactConfigSchema` (zod) in src/config/schema.mjs. Default behavior, when this block is omitted entirely, is the same as `{ enabled: true, categories: { all true }, entropyThreshold: 4.5, entropyMinLength: 24 }` as enforced at runtime by src/lib/secret-redactor.mjs.

categoryFlag.description: When false, the named category is excluded from redaction. Omitted keys default to true at runtime.

id.description: Stable identifier shown in `<REDACTED:id>` replacements and in observability hits.

pattern.description: JavaScript-flavored regular expression source. The runtime compiles this with the `g` flag.

replacement.description: Optional override; defaults to `<REDACTED:id>`. Use a length-stable replacement to keep suppression fingerprints from #687 stable.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, redaction is bypassed entirely; intended for one-off local debugging only.

extraPatterns.description: User-supplied additional patterns. Run AFTER the built-in named categories so users cannot accidentally weaken defaults.

allowlist.description: Substrings that, when found inside a candidate match, suppress redaction for that match. Used to protect documentation samples and intentional test fixtures.

denyFiles.description: Additional path globs that should be excluded from repo-wide context. Combined with `DEFAULT_DENY_GLOBS` from src/lib/secret-redactor.mjs.

entropyThreshold.description: Shannon entropy bits/char threshold for the high-entropy fallback. Lower values catch more tokens but raise false positives.

entropyMinLength.description: Minimum token length before the high-entropy fallback even considers a candidate.
